s.m. Door, gate; chapter, section, division (of a book), head, heading; subject, affair, business, topic, matter, particular, case; head of accounts, cess, tax; — postposn. In the matter (of, -ke), in the case (of), in respect (of), with regard (to), about
more: n. m. A. bāb door. 1. Division; section; chapter; heading. 2. Particular; point; affair; case; matter; business; object; topic. Hamāre bāb meṅ kyā hukm hai? 3. Tax; cess; head of accounts.
s. m. 1. A door, gate. 2. A chapter, section or division of a book. 3. Subject, head, affair, business, point, matter, reason, manner, mode, species, sort.
subs: bāb-yāft, s.m. Classified items entered (as fields, &c.) under their proper heads; — bāb-wārī, s.f. Classification.

ety: S. वप्रा or वप्रः; cf. bābū and bāp
def: s.m. Father; grandfather; old man, sir, sire (respectfully); a saṅyāsī, a faqīr; the head of the order of monks called Calendars or Qalandar; a form of address used by beggars in addressing the master of a house, and vice versâ; (amongst native servants of European masters), child, children
more: n. m. 1. A father; grandfather; old man; faqīr. Rūṭhe bābā ḍārhī hāth . Prov. The old man angry pulls his beard. 2. The head of an order of monks called qalandar . 3. A term of respect; sir; sire. 4. A form of address used by beggars towards the master of a house, & vice versā. Bābā kā bhalā ho! Benediction. 5. European children, generally so called by native servants. Bābā log Children.
s. m. Father, dad; sire! sir! child! باباجان bābā jān, Dear child!

def: To the wind.

def: adj. Relating to the lion or tiger (babar); leonine; tigerish; tiger-coloured.
s.f. Account, head, item (of account), article, business, affair, matter ( = bāb); — postposn. On account (of, -kī or -ke?), for, concerning, respecting, as to, in the matter (of)
more: prep. Ped. for H. maddhe . On account of; regarding; respecting; concerning; conversant with; touching; about; as to; in the matter of; in behalf of. uske bābat, jiske bābat . For which; concerning which; for the sake of.
n. f. 1. Account; statement; item of account. 2. Copy texts in join-hand, showing how ب is joined to all the letters of the alphabet.
s. f. Account, head, article, item; business, affair, matter; on account (of).
subs: s.f. Anything which is registered or entered in a general statement of accounts: bābat-yāft, s.f. The particular items of an account, classified items ( = bāb-yāft).

subs: (-kī), To take a kiss (from), to kiss.

ety: S. वप्रा or वप्र+उक; cf. bābā
def: s.m. Prince, noble, man of family or distinction; a title of respect (as) Sir, Mr., Esqre.; young master; father; a term of endearment applied to children; — a clerk or writer in an office.
more: n. m. S. वप्र. 1. A title of respect, as Sir, Mr., Esq., young master, father, Sire. 2. A man of family or distinction. Gurū hai hamārā, Rājā re bābū . Song. 3. A clerk or writer in an office. 4. A term of endearment applied to children. bābū bhaīyā , E. n. m. A gentleman; a man of rank and dignity. Bābū bhaīyā ke āge lajāë- ke chāhīṅ . E.
s. m. 1. A child. 2. A prince. 3. Master (a title given by Hindūs, equivalent to Mr. or squire).
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀩𑀸𑀩𑁆𑀩- (*bābba-), imitative of baby talk.

def: prop. n. Babel, or Babylon.

s.f. The gum-acacia tree, Mimosa arabica (the wood of which is commonly used in making cart-wheels, agricultural implements, tent-pegs, &c.; and the bark, being a powerful astringent, is used in tanning: syn. kīkar, mug̠īlān)
more: n. m. S. वर्वुर. Acacia arabica , the wood of which is much used in making cart wheels, agricultural implements, etc. and the bark is used in tanning leather, and in making wine ( kīkar ). Nadī kināre ghanī baburī re, ghanī baburī, Ghaṭā chhoṛ de, sipaiyā, bharūṅ gagrī . E. Wom. Song. Jhūmar . Fast by the river bank the acacia trees are thick, Pray, soldier, stand aside, I'll fill my pitcher quick. babūl ke peṛ bonā , v. a lit . To plant the babūl tree. To do something conductive to a bad end. Karnī kare to kyoṅ ḍare, kar-ke kyoṅ pachhtāë? Boë peṛ babūl ke, ām kahāṅ se khāë? Dohā.
s. f. The name of a tree of the Mimosa kind. (There are several species, but the commonest is Arabica. Roxb. ) The acacia tree.
subs: lit. 'To plant a babūl tree'; to do something conducive to harm or to a bad end.

more: n. m. H. bāo wind. A. whirlwind ( bagūlā ).

def: s.m. = baććā, q.v. s.m. Young (of any creature); child, infant, babe, little one; youngster, youth, lad, boy; thoughtless, simple or inexperienced person; — young plant, sapling
more: E; n. m. S. वत्स, Pr. vachchho the young of any animal. 1. The young of any creature; child; infant; babe; baby; nurseling; suckling ( dūd pītā bachchā ); bairn; little one; brat ( phūṅsṛā ); chit; urchin; bantling; bratling. 2. Youth; boy ( laṛkā ); lad; stripling; youngster; younker; whipster. Tū kāhe mor bachwā garyāvat rahī? E. Wom. Why did you abuse my child? 3. A thoughtless; inconsiderate; inexperienced person. 4. Chicken ( chūzah ); nestling ( chiṛyā kā bachchā ). 5. Sapling; seedling; young plant ( paudā ).

adj. 1. Child-like; innocent; simple. 2. Inexperienced; thoughtless ( abujh ); inconsiderate ( an-samajh ). bachche-bāzī, lauṅḍe bāzī , n. f. Sodomy. bachchoṅ kā khel, mūṅh kā nivālā, ghar kī bāt, nānī jī kā ghar . An easy matter; a trifle; a small affair. bachchoṅ kā sā , adj. Infantile; babyish; childish; girlish; puerile. bachche-kachche , n. m. Children; little ones. bachche-kashī , n. f. Child-bearing; pregnancy. bachche-kushī , P.; bāl-hattyā , H. n. f. Infanticide. adh-banā bachchā , n. A fœtus; embryo. bachche wālī mur/?/gī , n. f. lit . The hen with her chickens. The constellation of the Pleiades.
subs: s.m. Mere child's play, mere trifle, easy matter
bać-kać, s.m. Children, little ones ( = kaćće-baćće, kać-bać)
s.f. lit. 'Hen with chickens'; the Pleiades.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَچَّه (bačča), from Middle Persian wck' (waččag, “child, baby, cub”), from Proto-Iranian *wacáh (“calf”), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *watsás.

def: s.m. (pl. of baćća), Children, little ones.
adj. & adv. Fit for children; appertaining to children; child-like, puerile, childish; — in a childish manner.

adj. & s.m. = vāćak, q.v. adj. Speaking; — declaratory, explanatory, expressive (of), significant, implying, signifying; — verbal, expressed by words or sounds; — s.m. A significant sound, a word; (in Gram.) an explanatory particle; — a speaker; a reader; — a messenger.
more: part. act. 1. Speaker. 2. (in grammar) An explanatory particle.

def: (comp. of the porf. parts of baćnā and khućnā, q.q.v.)
s.m. Speech, word, expression, utterance, talk, discourse; declaration, affirmation, saying, precept, dictum, opinion; promise, assurance; agreement, compact, covenant, vow
more: n. m. S. वचन from वच् to speak, Pr. vaaṇam . 1. Speech; word ( bāt ); talk; discourse. Nikalte haiṅ ab to khushī ke bachan, Na ho gar khushī, to nahīṅ Barhaman . Mīr Hasan. A happy omen now appears upon your sky, If happiness be not your lot, no Brahman I. 2. Vow; solemn assurance; word of honor; parole ; pledge. Bachnoṅ kā bāṅdhā khaṛā hai . Prov. Saying. True to its pledge the sky stands firm. 3. Appointment; agreement ( iqrār ); promise. 4. Boon; favour. Kekaï ne do bachan māṅge. bachan-bandh honā , v. n. To give or pledge one's word. bachan-pāl , adj. True to one's word. bachan pālnā, yā nibhānā , v. n. To stand to one's bargain; to abide by a promise; to be true to one's word; to keep faith. bachan-patr , n. m. Deed; covenant; bond; agreement ( iqrār-nāmā ). bachan toṛ'nā, yā chhoṛnā , v. n. To break one's word or promise. bachan denā , v. a. To promise; engage; agree to; pledge one…

s. m. Speech, talk, discourse, word, expression; promise, agreement. بچن بندکرلينا bachan band-kar lenā or بچن بنده کرنا bachan bandh karnā, To bind by promise. بچن بنده هونا bachan bandh honā, To give oneʼs word, to promise, to be agreed upon. بچن پالنا bachan pālnā, To abide by a promise. بچن توڙنا bachan toṛnā, To break oneʼs word. بچن چهوڙنا bachan chhoṛnā, To break oneʼs promise. بچن دينا bachan denā, To promise, to agree. بچن ڐالنا bachan ḍālnā, To question, to ask, to inquire. بچن لينا bachan lenā, To receive a promise. بچن مارنا bachan mārnā, To conclude an agreement. بچن ماننا bachan mānnā, To obey. بچن نبهانا bachan nibhānā, To abide by a promise. بچن هارنا bachan hārnā, To promise, to agree, to give or pledge or plight oneʼs word, to affirm.
subs: adj. Bound by promise, &c., pledged; agreed upon, stipulated, &c.; — baćan-bandh karnā or kar-lenā, v.t. To bind (one) by promise, &c.; — baćan-bandh honā, v.n. To be bound by promise, &c. to give or pledge one's word, to promise; to be agreed upon
adj. Abiding by a promise; true to (one's) word; — baćan-pālnā, v.n. To keep (one's) word, abide by a promise, &c.

v.n. To conclude an agreement; to promise

(apnā), To give, pledge, or plight (one's) word; to promise; to agree to; to affirm, declare; — to break (one's) word or promise (a rare signification).

ety: S. वर्जयति rt. वृज् or वृच्, whence H. बच —
def: v.t. To save, preserve, rescue, deliver, extricate, set free; to sustain, support, protect; to secure, defend, guard, shield, screen, cloak; to conceal, hide; to set aside, put or lay by, save, spare, leave; to reserve, keep back (a part of anything fraudulently); to avoid, keep clear of, move out of the way
more: v. a. H. bīch ānā to come between. 1. To save; protect; shield; defend; ward off; beat off; preserve; keep. 1. Jisko Khudā bachāë, us par kabhī na āfat āë . Prov. On him whom God befriends no ill descends. 2. Hāth pair bachāīye, mūzū ko ṭarkāīye . Prov. Save yourself and spare the foe. 3. Āp ko bachātā hai, garīb ko phaṅsātā hai . Shields himself and snares the poor. 2. To maintain; support; sustain. 3. To screen; conceal; cover; hide. 4. To crib; embezzle; defraud; steal. Rupaë meṅ chār āne bachātā hai . 5. To assist; aid; help; to hold out a helping hand. 6. To extricate; deliver; free; liberate. 7. ( In law ). To set free; liberate; emancipate; redeem. 8. To lay by; save up; put by; hoard. Kaho, kuchh bachāte bhī ho, yā sab hī uṛā dete ho? 9. To clear the way; get out of the way. Chhakṛe-wāle, bachāīyo! Carter, look out! bachā jānā , v. a. To get off; escape; abscond. Tum har dafa gol…

v. a. 1. (trans. of بچنا) To save, to preserve, to protect, secure, to reserve, extricate, to spare, to defend, to guard, to leave, to avoid, to conceal. 2. (Perhaps, for بجانا q. v.) To sound (a musical instrument).
subs: v.n. To keep (oneself) clear of, to avoid, escape, get off scot free
(-ko), To carry off safe, to save, secure

When the mare is dead you may ride the filly. ☞ The unsuspecting license given by a Mullā or Divine to a wag who, under cover of the question put by him in the words of the proverb, obtained the religious sanction he wanted.

more: n. f. H. bāṅṭ divide. Subscription; proportionate rate; proportion of tax; rate of distribution; assessment on a share; portion; share. Bāṭī ghāṭī kutyā mare, nāth kahe merī bāchhā paṛe . Prov. ety: S. वर्जनीयं, rt. वृज् or वृच्
def: v.n. To be saved, to save oneself, escape (from, -se), effect (one's) escape; to be saved, spared, set aside, laid by; to remain over, be left; to be spared, to recover, survive, live; to turn away (from, -se), shrink (from); to avoid, shirk, fly (from)
more: v. n. विज् to separate. 1. To remain; to be over; to be left. Apne se bache, to aur ko deṅ . From needs of self whate'er is o'er may go to form another's store. 2. To be saved, spared, laid by. Kauṛī nahīṅ bachī, sārī tankhuāh ghāo ghap ho gaī . 3. To escape; make or effect one's escape; come out safe; to be saved. 1. Jachchā, bachchā donoṅ bacheṅ . Benediction. May the lying-in-mother and her child both live! 2. Jān bachī, lākhoṅ pāë . Prov. Escaped with life, millions found. 3. Ab-ke bache to ghar ghar rache . Prov. Spared this once, in every house the light of joy shall shine. 4. Yā Khudā khair! bachā hāth pair! Prov. Heaven preserve us, life and limb. 4. To recover; survive; live; to be alive. Bhādoṅ se bache to phir mileṅge . Prov. Escaped this August we shall meet again. 5. To escape from; shirk; elude; evade. 6. To hold back; keep from or aloof; abstain; eschew; avoid; shrink; run…
v. n. To be saved, be spared, to escape, avoid, remain unexpended, recover.
subs: v.n. To be or remain over; — to be on (one's) guard (against, -se), take care (against), avoid
v.n. To get clean away, escape in safety.

To contest a matter; contend for; bring an action ( muqadma laṛānā ). 3. Hin. Wom. To retort; give impertinent or crooked answers. Maiṅ kyā tere barābar kī hūṅ, jo mujh se bādā kartā hai . Wom. 4. To contend, engage, cope or fight with. Sapehrā bhasam lagāë āṅg ko, bidyā saṅg lāyā, Gauṛ Baṅgāle bīch bād karne kyoṅ āyā? Play. (imper. of بودن) Let it be, so be it.

v.n. To be changed or altered; to change, alter, vary; to assume another form, grow or become; to shift, veer, turn round; to be removed, transferred, transplanted; to change for the worse, look older; to lose colour, fade; to be disfigured; — v.t. To change, alter; to exchange, barter; to substitute one thing for another; to disguise; to place a false construction on, to misrepresent, pervert, torture, twist; to transfer, remove, transplant; to transform, transmute; to shift (one's ground, &c.), shuffle (generally with the word bāt, e.g. bāt badaltā hai)
more: v. n. 1. To change; alter; vary; shift; chop and change; veer; turn round. Hawā hī badal gaī . 2. To be changed; to assume another form; grow or become; turn into. 3. To be removed, transplanted, transferred. 4. To look worse, older, etc; lose color; to be disfigured. Tumhārī to bilkul sūrat hī badal gaī .

v. a. 1. To change; alter; make different; convert. 2. To barter; exchange. Lohe ke chane badal do . 3. To put one thing for another; substitute; commute. 4. To disguise; put a false coloring or construction upon. Bhes badalnā . 5. To transfer; transplant; transpose. Ek jagah se dūsrī jagah badalnā . 6. To transform; transmute. Sūrat badalnā . 7. To shuffle; shift; prevaricate; equivocate. Bāt badaltā hai? dānā badlī kar'nā , v. n. lit . To take the grain, as pigeons, out of each other's bills. Hence, to bill and coo.
v. a. To change, exchange, alter.
subs: v.n. = badalnā (and is the more common form)
v.t. To take in exchange, to exchange = badalnā.
wety: From بَدَل (badal) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدر (ba-dar), from به (ba) + در (dar).

def: s.m. Cotton; the plant Mimosa octandra; a certain medicinal drug.
s.f. The jujube tree (see badar); the cotton shrub; — name of one of the sources of the Ganges in the Himalaya range, a celebrated place of pilgrimage
subs: s.m. A kind of perfume, a leaf
s.f. Name of a plant, a variety of the Śephalica or Nyctanthes with blue flowers
s.f. A species of jujube tree; — a kind of perfume (apparently a dried shell-fish)
adv. In the direction of, or towards, Badrī

s.m. lit. 'Rock of Badarī'; name of a rocky eminence at the source of the Ganges ( = Badrī), the Badrī-nāth of modern travellers; or a town and temple on the west bank of the Alaka-nanda river in the province of Śrīnagar
s.m. Name of a temple at Badarī; also = badrī-śail, q.v.

def: s.f. The fruit of the jujube (see badar); — name of one of the sources of the Ganges, &c. ( = badrī, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Name of a hermitage, &c. ( = badrī-śail).

def: adv. For insertion or entry; by or according to the entry; per invoice.
adv. In or to a degree; by many degrees; ever-so-much, greatly, vastly
n. f. Writing off items of an account which are objectionable or excessive; audit of an account; taxing an overcharged bill.
s.f. See s.v. ba-dar. s.m. A large house; a granary raised on piles.
more: n. f. A drain; sewer. badar-rau-i-am , A public drain. badar-rau nikāl'nā, morī banānā , v. a. To make a drain or channel; drain; let out water, etc. badar nikālnā , v. a. To show a balance due; to debit.
s. f. A drain, a sewer.

def: adv. In hand; to hand.
adv. According to custom; according to rule or practice; in the usual manner, as usual, as before.
more: adv. According to rule or practice; as usual; as before.
As usual, according to custom, in the manner, customarily.

def: adv. By the good fortune (of, -kī), by the favour or bounty (of); by means (of), through, owing (to).
more: adv. By the grace or bounty of; under the auspices or good graces of; by means of; through; by dint of; owing to. Tumhārī ba-daulat roṭī khāte haiṅ .
adv. By favor of; by means of; under the auspices of. Āp kī ba-daulat roṭī kamā khāte haiṅ .
By the fortune, by the bounty, by the means: in or with empire or fortune.
wety: Compound of ب (ba) + دولت (daulat), both borrowed from Classical Persian.

def: s.m. A Brāhman; — submarine fire
adj. & s.m. Of the desert; — an Arab of the desert; a countryman, country-bumpkin.
more: adj. Of the country or desert; an Arab of the desert, a countryman.

def: s.f. Beginning, commencement.
— See s.v. بد for all comp. beginning with bad.
adj. 1. Ill-starred; unlucky; unfortunate. 2. Wretched; miserable; doomed; accursed.
Mah. Wom. n. m. A firebrand; mischief-maker; wretch. Paṛī rahtī hai thutkārī sī merī jān ke pīchhe, Mujhe jīne nahīṅ degī, bahan hai bad-balā terī . Wom. Rāhat.

n. m. A sail. bād-i-samūm , Ped.; H. lū , n. f. A hot wind like the blast from a furnace; the simoom. bād-i-mukhālif , n. f. Ped. for H. ulṭī hawā . Contrary winds. bād-i-muāfiq , Ped.; H. sīdhī hawā , n. f. Favourable winds.
more: s. m. 1. A sail. 2. A vessel used to hold a lamp for the purpose of protecting it from the wind.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بادبان (bādbān).

def: adj. Internally evil; false-hearted.
n. f. Bad or offensive smell; fetor; stink; stench ( dur-gandh ).
adj. Ill-conducted.
s. f. Bad conduct, misconduct.
n. f. Misdeeds; misdemeanour; unprincipled conduct; malpractices; bad conduct; misbehaviour.
s.m. Cloud = bādal, q.v. s.m. Cloud; sponge
more: (for بادل q. v.) s. m. A cloud.
subs: Clouds to disperse, to become clear (the sky)

def: n. m. S. वध् to kill. Slaughter; killing; murder; destruction. Āj kal Hindustān meṅ baṛā gaū-baddh hotā hai . Hin. Now-a-days there is great slaughter of cows.
more: n. f. S. विधि from विधा to arrange. 1. God ( bidhnā ). 2. The moon ( chāṅd ). Bidhi dāhine, dukkh binasāve, Vohī bāṅë hoë, dukh baḍhāve . Chaupāī. Bihārī Lāl. The moon upon your right, your pain is on the wane, But turned towards your left the moon increaseth pain. 3. A sacred precept; a law; rule; order; decree. 4. Custom; usage; manner; way; mode. 5. State; condition; case; situation. Jā bidhi rākhe Rām, tā hī bidh rahīye . Prov. Be content in the state in which Ram has placed you. 6. Kind; sort; make; fashion. 7. A conjunction of auspicious stars for the bride and bridegroom. 8. Balance of an account; adjustment of an account. bidh khā'nā yā mil'nā, mī'jān paṭnā v. n. 1. See ittifāq h . 2. To be struck (a bargain). bidh milānā , v. a. 1. To consult the stars, especially the horoscope of the bride and bridegroom before tying them in wedlock. Chalo hamāre sāth kahīṅ jā-ke tum bidh milwā…

n. m. S. बुद्धि from बुध् to know, Pālī. buddho . 1. Knowledge ( gyān ); intelligence; understanding ( samajh ); sense; intellect; perception; judgment; wisdom ( dānāī ). 2. Quickness of apprehension; discrimination; discernment; capacity ( buddhī ); cleverness; skill. Honhār hirde base, bisar jāt sub sudh, Jaisī hot hotabbitā, taisī upje budh . Dohā. The evil in our fate its shadow casts before, Its image occupies the mind, all sense forlore. 3. (S. बुद्ध ) A wise man; the ninth incarnation of Vishnu, and the apparent founder of Buddhism. 4. ( Ast .) Mercury (A. utārid ). 5. ( budh-wār ) Wednesday (P. chahārshambah ).
1. s. f. A sacred precept, rule, order, law, statute, direction, decree, injunction. 2. Manner, mode, kind, sort. 3. s. m. Name of Brahmā, providence.
s. f. Sense, knowledge, wisdom, understanding, intellect, discretion.
s. m. 1. A wise or learned man, a sage. 2. The planet Mercury. 3. Wednesday. ¶ बुद्ध buddha, The ninth avatār or Hindū incarnation.
wety: Shortened from بدھوار (budhvār), from Sanskrit बुधवार (budhavāra).

def: adj. Bound, tied; suppressed, checked, restrained, withheld, obstructed; fixed, firmly rooted
subs: (baddha+aṅjulī), s.f. Respectful salutation, putting the joined hands to the forehead by way of salutation
adj. & s.m. Young; — pupil; — child whose hair has not been shaven
s.f. Name of a pungent root, a kind of garlic
s.m. One who governs or suppresses his wrath

def: n. m. A pit. baḍ'ḍhā lag'nā , v. n. 1. To be dug, excavated, scooped out. 2. To be begun, commenced, set a going. 3. To begin, to be used, to lessen, diminish.
more: adj. S. वृध्द, Pālī buḍḍho . Old; advanced in years. buḍḍhā , n. m. An old man ( būṛhā ). Ham buḍḍhe huë to kyā, jī to nahīṅ buḍḍhā huā . Prov. Saying. What though I'm old in years, my heart's still young.

def: s.m. One who is bound; captive, prisoner.
n. m. Executioner; butcher; huntsman; fowler ( chiṛī-mār ). Jūṅ baddhak se gāë ḍare saë, so gat bhaī hamārī . Play. Chaubolā . I fear you as the ox fears the butcher.
s.f. See s.v. baddhā. adj. Bound, tied; suppressed, checked, restrained, withheld, obstructed; fixed, firmly rooted
subs: (baddha+aṅjulī), s.f. Respectful salutation, putting the joined hands to the forehead by way of salutation
adj. & s.m. Young; — pupil; — child whose hair has not been shaven
s.f. Name of a pungent root, a kind of garlic
s.m. One who governs or suppresses his wrath

def: s.f. An ornament worn round the neck, a long chain or garland of flowers worn (as by a bride and bridegroom) round the neck, hanging down to the waist, and crossing behind and before; a similar ornament made of leather; belt, sash; pair of braces; brace (of a carriage, &c.); streak, weal, mark of a stroke with a cane, &c.
more: s. f. An ornament worn round the neck, hanging down to the waist and crossing behind and before; a belt, a sash, brace (of a carriage, &c.).

def: adj. Dishonest ( dharoṛ- mār ); fraudulent ( dagā-bāz ); false.
n. f. Unfair dealing; fraud; dishonesty; corrupt practices. bad-diyānatī se , adv. Mala fide ; with fraudulent intent; dishonestly.
n. f. Curse; imprecation; malediction. bad-duā denā; H. sarāpnā , v. a. To curse; call down curses; heap curses on ( kosnā ).
wety: From بد (“mal-, evil, inauspicious”) + دعا (“prayer, wish”).

def: adv. Late; after a long time, after much delay.
Evil doer.

n. f. Speaking ill of one; slander; detraction; backbiting. Jo koī kare yāroṅ kī bad-goī, Uske bans meṅ rahe na koī! Rahe to koī andhā, laṅgṛā, lūlā, koṛhī! The prologue to a drinking bout of Bhaṅgaṛs . If any dare speak ill of us, May all his race be cut off thus, Or stricken blind, lame, maimed, leprous!
adj. Suspicious; mistrustful; disaffected. bad-gumānī, bad-zanī , n. f. Suspicion; mistrust; distrust.
s.m. Prohibition, hindrance, opposition, obstruction; annoyance; damage: danger; cord or tape of a bedstead ( = nawāṛ, &c.); smaller division of an estate; plain, desert.
s.f. Stubble.
s. f. Stubble.
s.m. Increase, augmentation, advance, progress, premium.
more: n. m. H. baḍhnā to increase. 1. Increase; premium; progress. Chār kauṛī bādhā . Delhi street cry. Four cowries' advance (on the market rate). 2. (S. वध् to hurt) Pain; anguish.
s. f. Pain, distress: obstruction, hinderance, prevention.
wety: From Sanskrit बाधा (bādhā).

def: s.f. Obstruction, impediment, &c. ( = bādh); refutation; pain, affliction, distress, anguish
subs: bādhā-kārī, adj. & s.m. Obstructive; obstructer, &c. = bādhak, q.v.
wety: From Sanskrit बाधा (bādhā).

def: s.m. Killing, slaying; death, murder, slaughter
subs: s.m. Punishment of death, capital punishment
badh-sthal, s.m. badh-sthalī, s.f. Place of execution, or of slaughter
s.f. Desire, or intent, to kill or hurt
adj. Wishing for death, desirous of dying
(-kā, or -ko), To kill, murder, slaughter ( = badhnā)
(badha+upāya), s.m. Instrument or means of putting to death
vulg. badhuʼans, s.m. Slaughter of a whole race or family; wholesale slaughter, butchery, utter destruction; — badh-waṅś karnā, v.t. To slaughter wholesale, to butcher, destroy, exterminate
adj. Deserving of death; fit to be killed or slaughtered.

def: s.f. = badhāwā, q.v. s.m. Congratulations on the birth of a child; presents distributed at the ceremony of cutting the umbilical cord, or at the birth of a child; presents carried to the house of a woman on the sixth or the fortieth day after childbirth; song of congratulation, congratulations; merry-making, revelry; — badhāwā denā, To congratulate, &c.
more: n. f. badhāvā ; Garh. baḍau , n. m. H. baṛhnā to increase. 1. Increase; growth; development (baṛhotrī) . 2. Propagation of the species (byāṅt) . 3. Child; offspring; hope of issue. Wom. 4. Congratulation on the birth of a child. 5. Revelry; enjoyment; pleasure ( maṅgal ). 6. Congratulations ( mubārkī ); a song of congratulation ( ānand-badhāvā ). Hin. Wom. Nand grah utre Kanhāī, Vā din ghar ghar bajī badhāī . Behāri Lāl. 7. Congratulatory gifts; presents to the servants at births and marriages. Ek ko sāī, dūsre ko badhāī . Prov. Earnest money to one, the job (or gift) to another. 8. Presents, etc. carried to the house of a woman on the sixth or fortieth day after childbirth ( badhāvā ). E. badhāī de'nā . v. a. To congratulate; to offer congratulations. badhāī gānā , v. a. To sing songs of congratulation.
wety: Borrowed from Hindi बधाई (badhāī).

def: s.f. Increase, growth, development, &c. ( = baṛhāʼī, q.v.); propagation; offspring, issue, child; hope of issue (in the language of women).
wety: Borrowed from Hindi बधाई (badhāī).

def: adj. & s.m. Obstructive, hindering; annoying; painful; damaging; — obstructer, opponent, foe; obstacle, hindrance, impediment.
s.m. One who kills, killer, slayer, slaughterer, butcher, executioner, murderer, assassin.
s.m. Opposing, hindering; obstruction, impeding; refutation; pain.
s.m. Killing, slaying, &c. ( = badh); see next.

v.t. To increase, &c. = baṛhānā, q.v. v.t. To increase, enlarge, extend, lengthen, produce, prolong, stretch, draw out; to advance, promote, raise, exalt, aggrandize; to amplify, magnify, exaggerate; to move forward, move on, carry forward, move away, remove (as a table-cloth), close (a shop, &c.), stop (a child's milk, e.g. dūdh baṛhānā, 'to wean'); to postpone, delay; to extinguish (a lamp)
more: (caus. of بدهنا) v. a. See بڙهانا baṛhānā.
subs: v.t. To bring forward, lead on (an army, &c.).

def: v.t. To sound, &c. = bajānā, q.v. v.t. To execute, &c. = ba-jā lānā, q.v. s.m. Musical instrument; instrumental music, music
subs: To play on a musical instrument; to strike up (music)
bājā-bajantar, s.m. Band of musicians, or of music ( = bajantarī)
s.m. Various musical in struments; sound or clanguor of many and various musical instruments (also bāj-gāj)
s.m. Maker or vendor of musical instruments; player on any instrument of music, musician ( = bājantarī).

def: s.m. Congratulations on the birth of a child; presents distributed at the ceremony of cutting the umbilical cord, or at the birth of a child; presents carried to the house of a woman on the sixth or the fortieth day after childbirth; song of congratulation, congratulations; merry-making, revelry; — badhāwā denā, To congratulate, &c.
more: s. m. 1. A song in congratulation. 2. Presents, &c. carried to the house of a woman on the sixth or fortieth day after childbirth.

def: s. f. Fruitlessness, uselessness, waste.
adj. 1. Wandering; vagrant; vagabond. 2. Nonsensical; unmeaning; senseless.
adj. 1. Stupefied; insensible; senseless. 2. Confused; confounded; bewildered; distracted. bad-hawās honā , v. n. To be confounded, etc.
n. f. 1. Stupefaction; insensibility; stupidity. 2. Consternation; distraction.
n. f. Indigestion.
s. f. Indigestion.
n. m. Capital punishment.
s.m. = baṛhelā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = bādhak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Obstructive, hindering; annoying; painful; damaging; — obstructer, opponent, foe; obstacle, hindrance, impediment.
s.m. = badhak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Obstructive, hindering; annoying; painful; damaging; — obstructer, opponent, foe; obstacle, hindrance, impediment.
s.m. Huntsman, sportsman, fowler; — killer, &c. ( = badhak, q.v.). adj. & s.m. Obstructive, hindering; annoying; painful; damaging; — obstructer, opponent, foe; obstacle, hindrance, impediment.
more: s. m. A huntsman, sportsman, fowler, killer, slayer, executioner.

def: adj. Deaf = bahrā, q.v. s.m. Property, fortune; share, portion, quota ( = baḵẖra, q.v.); profit, gain, advantage
subs: (-se), To possess a share (of), have part or lot (in); to be endowed (with)

adj. & s.m. Blessed, prosperous, fortunate, enjoying one's wish, contented, happy; profited; — one who is blessed, &c.; gainer, profiter; — bahra-mand karnā or kar-lenā (-ko, of person, -se, of thing), To make (one) possessed (of), put (one) in possession (of), to gratify (one, with a thing, &c.)
(-se), To be in possession (of), to participate, share; to derive benefit (from); to accomplish an object, be fortunate, to prosper
adj. & s.m. Partaking; profited; profiting; — partaker, sharer; gainer.

def: s.f. Deafness = bahrā-pan.
part. Obstructed, impeded; pained, afflicted, tormented.
s.m. A disease affecting jawār, bājrā, Indian corn, and sugar-cane, which prevents the head from shooting.
s.f. A castrated animal; a bullock; gelding
more: n. f. S. वध् to hurt. A castrated animal ( ākhtā ). badhiyā baiṭh'nā , (Slang) v. a. To be bankrupt or insolvent. Hazār rupaë kī badhiyā baiṭh gaī . A thousand rupees' bull is gone. badhiyā k . v. a. See a/?/khtah karnā .
s. f. A bullock, any castrated animal.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀤𑁆𑀥𑀺𑀅 (vaddhia), from Sanskrit वध्रि (vádhri, “castrated”).

def: v.t. To oppose, hinder, obstruct, resist, check, thwart, frustrate.
v.t. To cut, divide, cut off; to shear.

ety: badh˚ = Prk. बध(इ) = S. बाध(ते), rt. बध् or बाध्
def: v.t. To kill, slay, slaughter; to smite; to wound.
more: v. a. To kill ( mār ḍālnā ); murder; slaughter; butcher; destroy.

Hin. n. m. badhnī , Dim. n. f. An earthen or copper pot with a spout to it; a vessel for drinking water from. be-peṅdī kā badhnā , n. m. (Slang). A fickle or inconsistent person. be-ṭoṅṭī kā badhnā , n. m. Fac. An hermaphrodite; a eunuch.
v. a. To smite, kill. ¶ bidhnā (r. विध or intransitive of بيدهنا) v.n. To be pierced or perforated.
s. m. A kind of pot with a spout to it, a vessel for drinking water from.
v. n. (dakh.) To increase, to grow: see بڙهنا baṛhnā.

def: v.t. = badnā, q.v. v.n. To speak, discourse; to dispute, argue, wrangle, &c. ( = bād karnā, q.v.s.v. bād); to play on an instrument; to sound, strike (as a clock).
v.n. To increase grow, &c. = baṛhnā, q.v.
s.m. An earthen or metal (particularly copper) vessel with a spout to it; a vessel for drinking water from.
s.f. Dim. of badhnā, 'a vessel,' q.v.
more: s. f. A small pot with a spout to it: see بدهنا badhnā.

def: s.m. See s.v. badh. s.m. Prohibition, hindrance, opposition, obstruction; annoyance; damage: danger; cord or tape of a bedstead ( = nawāṛ, &c.); smaller division of an estate; plain, desert.
s.f. Newly-married woman, bride; young wife; daughter-in-law, son's wife; wife; woman, female (see bahū).
more: n. m. (Slang.) A dunce; idiot; ass ( ullū 2). 1. Kānā taṭṭū, buddhū nafar . Prov. A blind horse, a foolish groom. 2. Chal buddhū morī kī rāh. Mazāq . Jest. Go along, blockhead, the drain's your way.
s. f. A wife, daughter-in-law, woman, lady.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀩𑀼𑀤𑁆𑀥 (*buddha). Unrelated to بُدھ (budh, “Buddha; wisdom”).

def: s.m. See s.v. badh. s.m. Prohibition, hindrance, opposition, obstruction; annoyance; damage: danger; cord or tape of a bedstead ( = nawāṛ, &c.); smaller division of an estate; plain, desert.
adj. Requiring to be checked, stopped, &c. (see bādh).
part. adj. Requiring to be killed, fit to be slaughtered; deserving of death; condemned or doomed to death
subs: s.f. badhya-sthān, s.m. Place of slaughter or execution (badh-sthān).

def: s.f. Fitness to be killed or put to death.

s.m. Speaking, discoursing, asserting, affirming, &c.; speaker, propounder, debater, arguer, disputant; accuser, complainant, plaintiff, suitor, prosecutor; mischief-maker; enemy; (in Hindū theology), expounder of the laws and sacred writings, a sage; (in music), key-note
more: Displaced by A. مدعي n. m. 1. Plaintiff; complainant; suitor. 2. A wicked person.
n. f. Rheumatism; flatulence. bādī phailānā , v. n. 1. To induce indolence or idleness by yawning, or any diversion, etc. 2. To make a row; create a disturbance.
s. m. A speaker, an accuser, a plaintiff, an enemy, disputant, arguer. بادي چور bādī-chor, An inveterate thief.

def: part. (of بدو 'to appear'), Appearing, beginning; beginner, author; first (in comp.)
subs: adv. At the first glance, at first sight ( = dekhte-hī).

def: s.f. = badī, q.v. s.m. Speaking, discoursing, asserting, affirming, &c.; speaker, propounder, debater, arguer, disputant; accuser, complainant, plaintiff, suitor, prosecutor; mischief-maker; enemy; (in Hindū theology), expounder of the laws and sacred writings, a sage; (in music), key-note
more: s. f. The wane of the moon See S̱. بدي.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بد (bad). Unrelated to English bad.

def: s.f. Badness, wickedness, evil, ill, mischief, injury, misfortune
subs: v.n. To be bent on mischief, &c.
(-kī), To do wrong or harm (to), to injure, hurt; to speak ill (of, -kī), malign, slander, backbite.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَدِی (badī). By surface analysis, ِبَد (ibad) + ی (ī).

def: adj. Novel, wonderful, strange, rare, curious
more: (from بدع) adj. Novel, wonderful, rare, curious, strange.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَدِیع (badī'), itself from Arabic بَدِيع (badīʕ).

def: adj. & s.f. Rheumatic; producing or causing flatulence; cold and damp; — rheumatism; flatulence; anything of a baneful or mischievous character, mischief, disturbance, calamity
subs: To spread mischief, &c.; to cause a disturbance; to cause or induce indolence or inertness (by yawning, or any diversion)

def: s.f. The dark half of the lunar month, from full moon to new moon; the wane of the moon.
more: n. f. Opp. of sudī , S. वदि. The fortnight of the waning moon; the dark fortnight ( aṅdherā pākh ).

n. f. Opp. of nekī . Badness. See بُراي Nekī kā samrā badī . Prov. Evil for good. badī par ānā, dil meṅ badī samā'nā,—lānā , v. n. To be bent on mischief; to be full of mischief; to become refractory or restive. Mujh se badī lāë to khūb ṭhīk banāūṅgā. badī chītnā , W; badī chetnā , E. v. n. To think ill of one. badī kar'nā , v. a. 1. To do evil or wrong. 2. To injure; hurt, etc. 3. To speak ill of; backbite; speak against. Kisī kī badī tū na kar, aib hai, Ki uskā Khudā ālim-ul-gaib hai . Mīr Hasan. Speak ill of none, it is a sin, God only knows what's hid within.
s. f. The dark half of the lunar month from full moon to new moon.
(from بد) s. f. Badness, wickedness, evil, ill, mischief, injury, misfortune.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَدِی (badī). By surface analysis, ِبَد (ibad) + ی (ī).

def: s.f. = baddhī, q.v. s.f. An ornament worn round the neck, a long chain or garland of flowers worn (as by a bride and bridegroom) round the neck, hanging down to the waist, and crossing behind and before; a similar ornament made of leather; belt, sash; pair of braces; brace (of a carriage, &c.); streak, weal, mark of a stroke with a cane, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَدِی (badī). By surface analysis, ِبَد (ibad) + ی (ī).

def: n. m. A great, determined or inveterate thief. Le hī jātī hai zar-i-gul ko uṛā, Subah kī bhī bāo bādī chor hai . Mīr. The golden pollen of the blooming flow'r you find, Borne by swift-footed thief of morn, the early wind. bādī kā badan , n. m. lit . A body swollen with wind. An unwieldy mass of flesh; corpulence.
s.m. Anything done impromptu, or without premeditation or consideration; an extempore, an impromptu; an unexpected event; an axiom.
s. m. 1. An extempore, an impromptu, any thing done without meditation, an unexpected event. 2. An axiom.
adj. Impromptu, extempore, unpremeditated; apparent, obvious, manifest, self-evident; — s.f. An axiom.
1. adj. Unpremeditated, apparent, self-evident. 2. s. f. An axiom.
Ped. adj. بَدَهَهُ It happened to him suddenly. 1. Obvious; evident; manifest; self-evident. 2. Spontaneous; unpremediated; extempore. amar-i-badīh ; H. khulī bāt , n. An obvious fact; an axiom.
adv. With trouble or difficulty
